title: గంగా తీరాన గొప్ప స్నేహం
moral: నిస్వార్థమైన సేవ, స్వచ్ఛమైన భక్తి మన జీవితంలో అపురూపమైన బంధాలను, అంతులేని ఆనందాన్ని ఇస్తాయి.
main_character: Guha, the Nishada king. A man of the forest and river, with a strong, weathered build from years of rowing. His skin is tanned brown from the sun, and his dark hair is often tied back simply. His eyes are kind and observant, reflecting a deep respect for the Ganga and all who travel it. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The banks of the sacred Ganga river near Shringaverapura, a village surrounded by dense forest. The river flows wide and calm, with small boats docked at the muddy shore, and ancient banyan trees casting long shadows. A warm, humid afternoon light often filters through the leaves. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: గంగానది ఒడ్డున గుహుడు, తన పడవను పక్కన పెట్టి, ప్రశాంతంగా ప్రవహిస్తున్న జలాలను చూస్తూ కూర్చున్నాడు. నీళ్ళు మెల్లగా పడవలను తాకుతుంటే, తడి మట్టి, రెల్లుగడ్డి సువాసన అతని మనసును ప్రశాంతంగా నిమరింది. ఇంతలో ఒక దూత హడావిడిగా వచ్చి, రాముడు అరణ్యవాసానికి వెళ్ళాడని చెప్పాడు — ఆ మాట వినగానే గుహుడి మొహం ఒక్కసారిగా మారిపోయింది. అతని చేతిలో ఉన్న తెడ్డును గట్టిగా పట్టుకున్నాడు — వేళ్ళు తెల్లబడ్డాయి. "రాముడు అరణ్యవాసానికి వెళ్ళాడంటే నమ్మలేకున్నాను!" అని పెదవులు వణకగా అన్నాడు. తన ప్రియమైన రాజు కోసం ఏదైనా చేయాలని అతని హృదయం తపించిపోయింది — వెంటనే నది అవతలి ఒడ్డు వైపు ఆత్రంగా కళ్ళు పరుగెత్తించాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The man sits by the river, gripping an oar tightly in his hands. His knuckles are white as his lips tremble, having just heard shocking news. Late afternoon sun, warm golden light reflecting off the wide, calm surface of the Ganga, casting long shadows from the banyan trees on the riverbank.
scenes.scene_visual: The man sits by the river, gripping an oar tightly in his hands. His knuckles are white as his lips tremble, having just heard shocking news.
scenes.text_te_en: Gangaanadi Odduna Guhudu, Tana Padavanu Pakkana Petti, Prashaantangaa Pravahistunna Jalaalanu Chustu Kurchunnaadu. Nillu Mellagaa Padavalanu Taakutunte, Tadi Matti, Rellugaddi Suvaasana Atani Manasunu Prashaantangaa Nimarindi. Intalo Oka Duta Hadaavidigaa Vachchi, Raamudu Aranyavaasaaniki Vellaadani Cheppaadu — Aa Maata Vinagaane Guhudi Moham Okkasaarigaa Maaripoyindi. Atani Chetilo Unna Teddunu Gattigaa Pattukunnaadu — Vellu Tellabaddaayi. "Raamudu Aranyavaasaaniki Vellaadante Nammalekunnaanu!" Ani Pedavulu Vanakagaa Annaadu. Tana Priyamaina Raaju Kosam Edainaa Cheyaalani Atani Hrridayam Tapinchipoyindi — Ventane Nadi Avatali Oddu Vaipu Aatrangaa Kallu Parugettinchaadu.
scenes.text_en: By the banks of the Ganga river, Guhudu sat with his boat beside him, watching the peaceful waters flow. The water gently touched the boats, and the smell of wet earth and reeds calmed his mind. Suddenly, a messenger rushed over and said that Ramudu had gone to live in the forest. As soon as Guhudu heard this, his face changed completely. He gripped the oar in his hand tightly – his fingers turned white. "I can't believe Ramudu has gone to the forest!" he said, his lips trembling. His heart longed to do something for his beloved king. Immediately, his eyes eagerly scanned the other side of the river.

scenes.text: గుహుడు ఆత్రంగా ఎదురుచూస్తున్న సమయంలో, అడవి మార్గం నుండి రామలక్ష్మణులు, సీతాదేవితో కలిసి వచ్చారు — వారి అడుగుల చప్పుడు కింద రాలిన ఎండుటాకులను మెల్లగా కదిపింది. వారిని చూడగానే గుహుడి కళ్ళు ఆనందంతో మెరిశాయి, వెంటనే దుఃఖంతో చెమ్మగిల్లాయి. అతను తన స్థానం నుండి వేగంగా లేచి, వారికి ఎదురుగా పరిగెత్తాడు — అతని కాళ్ళు భూమిని తాకడం లేదు. వారి కష్టాన్ని చూసి అతని గుండె బరువెక్కింది, చేతులు వాటంతట అవే జోడించుకున్నాయి. "స్వామీ, నా ఆతిథ్యం స్వీకరించండి! నా ఇల్లు మీదే!" అని గద్గద స్వరంతో పలికాడు. కానీ రాముడి మొహం ప్రశాంతంగా, ఏదో నిర్ణయించుకున్నట్లు కనిపించింది — గుహుడి ఆతిథ్యాన్ని మర్యాదగా తిరస్కరించడానికి సిద్ధంగా.
scenes.image_prompt: The man rushes forward from his spot, his hands instinctively folded in greeting. His eyes are shining with a mix of joy and sorrow as he approaches the three figures. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Soft, diffused light filtering through the dense forest canopy, creating a dappled pattern on the ground, a sense of quiet solemnity.
scenes.scene_visual: The man rushes forward from his spot, his hands instinctively folded in greeting. His eyes are shining with a mix of joy and sorrow as he approaches the three figures.
scenes.text_te_en: Guhudu Aatrangaa Eduruchustunna Samayanlo, Adavi Maargam Nundi Raamalakshmanulu, Sitaadevito Kalisi Vachchaaru — Vaari Adugula Chappudu Kinda Raalina Endutaakulanu Mellagaa Kadipindi. Vaarini Chudagaane Guhudi Kallu Aanandanto Merishaayi, Ventane Duhkhanto Chemmagillaayi. Atanu Tana Sthaanam Nundi Vegangaa Lechi, Vaariki Edurugaa Parigettaadu — Atani Kaallu Bhumini Taakadam Ledu. Vaari Kashtaanni Chusi Atani Gunde Baruvekkindi, Chetulu Vaatantata Ave Jodinchukunnaayi. "Svaami, Naa Aatithyam Svikarinchandi! Naa Illu Mide!" Ani Gadgada Svaranto Palikaadu. Kaani Raamudi Moham Prashaantangaa, Edo Nirnayinchukunnatlu Kanipinchindi — Guhudi Aatithyaanni Maryaadagaa Tiraskarinchadaaniki Siddhangaa.
scenes.text_en: While Guhudu was eagerly waiting, Ramudu and Lakshmanulu, along with Seetadevi, came from the forest path. The sound of their footsteps gently rustled the dry leaves on the ground. As soon as Guhudu saw them, his eyes sparkled with joy, then immediately filled with tears of sorrow. He quickly rose from his spot and ran towards them – his feet barely touching the ground. His heart felt heavy seeing their hardship, and his hands automatically joined in respect. "My Lord, please accept my hospitality! My home is yours!" he said with a trembling voice. But Ramudu's face looked calm, as if he had made a decision – ready to politely refuse Guhudu's offer.

scenes.text: రాముడు గుహుడి వైపు ప్రేమగా చూస్తూ, తన వనవాస నియమాన్ని వివరించాడు — వారి సంభాషణకు నేపథ్యంగా దగ్గరలోని పొదల్లోంచి కీటకాల సన్నని శబ్దం నిరంతరాయంగా వినిపించింది. "నేను అరణ్యవాసిని, రాజ భోగాలు నాకు వద్దు," అని రాముడు మెల్లగా, కానీ దృఢంగా అన్నాడు. ఆ మాటలు వినగానే గుహుడి భుజాలు కొద్దిగా వాలాయి — అతని ఆశలు నిరాశగా మారినట్లు. కానీ వెంటనే అతని కళ్ళు విశాలమయ్యాయి, ఒక కొత్త ఆలోచన మెదడులో మెరిసింది — రాముడికి సేవ చేసే మరో మార్గం దొరికినట్లు. "స్వామీ, మీరు గంగానదిని దాటడానికి నేను సహాయం చేస్తాను," అని గుహుడు ఉత్సాహంగా పలికాడు. వెంటనే రాముడి పాదాల దగ్గర మోకరిల్లి, తన హృదయంలోని కోరికను విన్నవించడానికి సిద్ధమయ్యాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The man kneels down at the feet of the royal figure, his shoulders having just slumped in disappointment. His eyes are now wide with a new, enthusiastic idea, ready to make a request. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Mid-morning sun breaking through the trees, casting bright shafts of light onto the riverbank, highlighting the expressions of the characters.
scenes.scene_visual: The man kneels down at the feet of the royal figure, his shoulders having just slumped in disappointment. His eyes are now wide with a new, enthusiastic idea, ready to make a request.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu Guhudi Vaipu Premagaa Chustu, Tana Vanavaasa Niyamaanni Vivarinchaadu — Vaari Sambhaashanaku Nepathyangaa Daggaraloni Podallonchi Kitakaala Sannani Shabdam Nirantaraayangaa Vinipinchindi. "Nenu Aranyavaasini, Raaja Bhogaalu Naaku Vaddu," Ani Raamudu Mellagaa, Kaani Drridhangaa Annaadu. Aa Maatalu Vinagaane Guhudi Bhujaalu Koddigaa Vaalaayi — Atani Aashalu Niraashagaa Maarinatlu. Kaani Ventane Atani Kallu Vishaalamayyaayi, Oka Kotta Aalochana Medadulo Merisindi — Raamudiki Seva Chese Maro Maargam Dorikinatlu. "Svaami, Miru Gangaanadini Daatadaaniki Nenu Sahaayam Chestaanu," Ani Guhudu Utsaahangaa Palikaadu. Ventane Raamudi Paadaala Daggara Mokarilli, Tana Hrridayanloni Korikanu Vinnavinchadaaniki Siddhamayyaadu.
scenes.text_en: Ramudu looked at Guhudu with love and explained his vow of living in the forest. In the background of their conversation, the soft sound of insects from nearby bushes could be heard continuously. "I am a forest dweller now, I do not need royal comforts," Ramudu said softly but firmly. As soon as Guhudu heard these words, his shoulders drooped a little – as if his hopes had turned into disappointment. But immediately, his eyes widened, and a new idea sparked in his mind – as if he had found another way to serve Ramudu. "My Lord, I will help you cross the Ganga river," Guhudu said enthusiastically. He immediately knelt at Ramudu's feet, ready to express the wish in his heart.

scenes.text: గుహుడి కోరికను రాముడు మన్నించగానే, గుహుడి కళ్ళల్లో ఆనంద భాష్పాలు నిలిచాయి — అతని పెదాలపై చిరునవ్వు విరిసింది. అతను తన చేతులతో చల్లటి, స్వచ్ఛమైన గంగాజలాన్ని తెచ్చి, రాముడి, సీతమ్మ, లక్ష్మణుల పాదాలను భక్తిశ్రద్ధలతో కడిగాడు — నీళ్ళు అతని చేతులపై మెల్లగా చిందాయి. "మీ పాదాలను కడగడం నా జీవిత భాగ్యం!" అని అతని కంఠం ప్రేమతో వణికింది. ఆ తర్వాత వారిని తన చిన్న పడవలో ఎక్కించుకుని, గంగానదిని దాటించడం మొదలుపెట్టాడు — తెడ్డుల చప్పుడు లయబద్ధంగా వినిపిస్తోంది. అతను తెడ్డు వేస్తుంటే, అతని గుండె సంతోషంతో నిండిపోయింది — తన జీవితంలో ఇంత గొప్ప సేవ చేయగలిగినందుకు పులకించిపోయాడు. అవతలి ఒడ్డుకు చేరుకోగానే, రాముడు గుహుడి వైపు ప్రేమగా, కృతజ్ఞతతో చూశాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The man gently washes the feet of the three royal figures with cool, clear river water. A small smile plays on his lips, and tears of joy well up in his eyes as water splashes softly over his hands. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Bright, clear daylight over the open river, the sun glinting off the water, reflecting the purity and significance of the act.
scenes.scene_visual: The man gently washes the feet of the three royal figures with cool, clear river water. A small smile plays on his lips, and tears of joy well up in his eyes as water splashes softly over his hands.
scenes.text_te_en: Guhudi Korikanu Raamudu Manninchagaane, Guhudi Kallallo Aananda Bhaashpaalu Nilichaayi — Atani Pedaalapai Chirunavvu Virisindi. Atanu Tana Chetulato Challati, Svachchamaina Gangaajalaanni Techchi, Raamudi, Sitamma, Lakshmanula Paadaalanu Bhaktishraddhalato Kadigaadu — Nillu Atani Chetulapai Mellagaa Chindaayi. "Mi Paadaalanu Kadagadam Naa Jivita Bhaagyan!" Ani Atani Kantham Premato Vanikindi. Aa Tarvaata Vaarini Tana Chinna Padavalo Ekkinchukuni, Gangaanadini Daatinchadam Modalupettaadu — Teddula Chappudu Layabaddhangaa Vinipistondi. Atanu Teddu Vestunte, Atani Gunde Santoshanto Nindipoyindi — Tana Jivitanlo Inta Goppa Seva Cheyagaliginanduku Pulakinchipoyaadu. Avatali Odduku Cherukogaane, Raamudu Guhudi Vaipu Premagaa, Krritajnyatato Chushaadu.
scenes.text_en: As soon as Ramudu granted Guhudu's wish, tears of joy welled up in Guhudu's eyes – a smile bloomed on his lips. He brought cool, pure Ganga water in his hands and devoutly washed the feet of Ramudu, Seetamma, and Lakshmanulu – the water gently splashed on his hands. "It is my life's fortune to wash your feet!" his voice trembled with love. After that, he helped them into his small boat and began to cross the Ganga river – the rhythmic sound of the oars could be heard. As he rowed, his heart filled with happiness – he was thrilled to be able to perform such a great service in his life. Upon reaching the other bank, Ramudu looked at Guhudu with love and gratitude.

scenes.text: అవతలి ఒడ్డుకు చేరుకోగానే, రాముడు గుహుడిని దగ్గరకు తీసుకుని, అతని వీపు తట్టాడు — ఆ క్షణం గుహుడికి స్వర్గం కళ్ళ ముందు కనిపించింది. అడవిలోని మల్లెపూల తీయని సువాసనను మోసుకుంటూ ఒక చల్లని గాలి వారిని తాకింది. "ఈ రోజు నుండి నీవు నాకు సోదరుడవు, నా ప్రియ మిత్రుడవు," అని రాముడు ప్రేమగా పలికాడు, అతని కళ్ళల్లో ఆప్యాయత తొణికిసలాడింది. ఆ మాటలు వినగానే గుహుడి మోకాళ్ళు వణికాయి, అతని కళ్ళలో ఆనంద బాష్పాలు ధారగా కారాయి — అతను ఒక్క మాట కూడా మాట్లాడలేకపోయాడు. తన ప్రభువు తనను సోదరుడిగా అంగీకరించాడని తెలిసినప్పుడు, అతని హృదయం కృతజ్ఞతతో పొంగిపోయింది. రాముడు, సీత, లక్ష్మణులు అడవిలోకి వెళ్ళిపోతుండగా, గుహుడు వారిని కళ్ళు చెదరనీయకుండా చూశాడు — వారి రూపాలు మెల్లగా కనుమరుగయ్యాయి.
scenes.image_prompt: The man stands trembling, his knees weak, as tears stream down his face. He is speechless, overwhelmed with gratitude after being embraced and called a brother by the royal figure. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Soft, golden light of late afternoon filtering through the trees at the edge of the forest, creating a warm, poignant glow around the departing figures.
scenes.scene_visual: The man stands trembling, his knees weak, as tears stream down his face. He is speechless, overwhelmed with gratitude after being embraced and called a brother by the royal figure.
scenes.text_te_en: Avatali Odduku Cherukogaane, Raamudu Guhudini Daggaraku Tisukuni, Atani Vipu Tattaadu — Aa Kshanam Guhudiki Svargam Kalla Mundu Kanipinchindi. Adaviloni Mallepula Tiyani Suvaasananu Mosukuntu Oka Challani Gaali Vaarini Taakindi. "I Roju Nundi Nivu Naaku Sodarudavu, Naa Priya Mitrudavu," Ani Raamudu Premagaa Palikaadu, Atani Kallallo Aapyaayata Tonikisalaadindi. Aa Maatalu Vinagaane Guhudi Mokaallu Vanikaayi, Atani Kallalo Aananda Baashpaalu Dhaaragaa Kaaraayi — Atanu Okka Maata Kudaa Maatlaadalekapoyaadu. Tana Prabhuvu Tananu Sodarudigaa Angikarinchaadani Telisinappudu, Atani Hrridayam Krritajnyatato Pongipoyindi. Raamudu, Sita, Lakshmanulu Adaviloki Vellipotundagaa, Guhudu Vaarini Kallu Chedaraniyakundaa Chushaadu — Vaari Rupaalu Mellagaa Kanumarugayyaayi.
scenes.text_en: As soon as they reached the other bank, Ramudu embraced Guhudu and patted his back – at that moment, Guhudu felt like he was in heaven. A cool breeze, carrying the sweet scent of jasmine flowers from the forest, touched them. "From this day forward, you are my brother, my dear friend," Ramudu said lovingly, his eyes filled with affection. As soon as Guhudu heard these words, his knees trembled, and tears of joy streamed from his eyes – he couldn't speak a single word. When he realized his Lord had accepted him as a brother, his heart overflowed with gratitude. As Ramudu, Seeta, and Lakshmanulu walked into the forest, Guhudu watched them without blinking – their figures slowly disappeared.

scenes.text: అనేక సంవత్సరాల తర్వాత, దశరథ పుత్రులు తిరిగి వస్తున్నారన్న వార్త శ్రింగవేరపురం చేరింది — దూరంగా శంఖారావాలు, విజయ దుందుభులు మారుమోగాయి. గుహుడు ఆ వార్త వినగానే, అతని మొహం ఆనందంతో ప్రకాశించింది — అతని హృదయం రామ నామంతో నిండిపోయింది. అతను మళ్ళీ గంగానది ఒడ్డుకు పరిగెత్తాడు, రాముడిని చూడాలనే తపనతో. రాముడు రాగానే, గుహుడు అతన్ని గట్టిగా కౌగిలించుకున్నాడు — వారి బంధం చెక్కుచెదరలేదని రుజువు చేస్తూ. "రామా, నీ రాక కోసం ఎంతగానో ఎదురుచూశాను!" అని గుహుడు కళ్ళల్లో నీళ్లతో పలికాడు. తన సేవకు లభించిన ఈ అపురూపమైన భాగ్యం తన జీవితాన్ని సంతోషంతో నింపిందని అతని మనసు పులకించిపోయింది.
scenes.image_prompt: The man tightly embraces the royal figure, his face shining with immense joy. Tears well up in his eyes as he speaks, having waited many years for this reunion. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Bright, celebratory morning light, vibrant and full of joy, illuminating the royal procession and the welcoming crowd.
scenes.scene_visual: The man tightly embraces the royal figure, his face shining with immense joy. Tears well up in his eyes as he speaks, having waited many years for this reunion.
scenes.text_te_en: Aneka Sanvatsaraala Tarvaata, Dasharatha Putrulu Tirigi Vastunnaaranna Vaarta Shringaverapuram Cherindi — Durangaa Shankhaaraavaalu, Vijaya Dundubhulu Maarumogaayi. Guhudu Aa Vaarta Vinagaane, Atani Moham Aanandanto Prakaashinchindi — Atani Hrridayam Raama Naamanto Nindipoyindi. Atanu Malli Gangaanadi Odduku Parigettaadu, Raamudini Chudaalane Tapanato. Raamudu Raagaane, Guhudu Atanni Gattigaa Kaugilinchukunnaadu — Vaari Bandham Chekkuchedaraledani Rujuvu Chestu. "Raamaa, Ni Raaka Kosam Entagaano Eduruchushaanu!" Ani Guhudu Kallallo Nillato Palikaadu. Tana Sevaku Labhinchina I Apurupamaina Bhaagyam Tana Jivitaanni Santoshanto Nimpindani Atani Manasu Pulakinchipoyindi.
scenes.text_en: Many years later, the news that Dasharatha's sons were returning reached Shringaverapuram. In the distance, the sounds of conch shells and victory drums echoed. As soon as Guhudu heard the news, his face lit up with joy – his heart filled with Ramudu's name. He ran to the bank of the Ganga river again, eager to see Ramudu. As soon as Ramudu arrived, Guhudu hugged him tightly – proving that their bond was unbroken. "Ramudu, I have waited so long for your return!" Guhudu said with tears in his eyes. His mind was thrilled that this rare fortune of serving Ramudu had filled his life with happiness.

supporting_characters.description: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Always the same character across every scene.

Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with jasmine flowers. Wearing a silk saree in deep red or golden hues, with traditional gold ornaments: necklace, bangles, nose ring, and earrings. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, composed expression. Always the same character across every scene.

Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Always the same character across every scene.

Bharata: a young prince with a fair complexion, wearing a simple white dhoti and minimal ornaments (he lives in austerity while Rama is in exile). Gentle, grief-stricken yet dignified expression. Carries Rama's sandals (paduka) as a symbol of his brother's authority. Always the same character across every scene.

quality_scores.notes: The story effectively conveys Guha's devotion and the profound bond formed with Rama, using vivid descriptions and strong emotional showing. The narrative arc is compelling, demonstrating a clear struggle and a deeply satisfying resolution. To elevate this to an award-worthy piece, the most crucial improvement would be to enrich the dialogue. Expanding character-specific conversations would not only deepen character voices but also allow the story to unfold more dynamically through interaction, rather than relying heavily on narration.

moral_te_en: Nisvaarthamaina Seva, Svachchamaina Bhakti Mana Jivitanlo Apurupamaina Bandhaalanu, Antuleni Aanandaanni Istaayi.
title_en: A Great Friendship by the River Ganga
moral_en: Selfless help and pure love bring us special friendships and endless happiness in our lives.
topic: కేవటుని నిస్వార్థ భక్తి - స్వచ్ఛమైన సేవకు లభించిన భాగ్యం
